SEARCH
Package server.dao

Class AuthDAO

java.lang.Object
server.dao.AuthDAO
public class AuthDAO extends Object
Data access object for authentication: staff/visitor credential lookups and the single-login lock backed by the active_session table.

Follows the project's standard DAO conventions: each method opens a short-lived Connection from DBConnection, runs parameterized statements, and maps rows to DTOs. SQL exceptions are logged and surfaced as a null/false return rather than being propagated, so callers signal failure through the return value.

  • Constructor Details

    • AuthDAO

      public AuthDAO()
      Creates the authentication DAO.

  • Method Details

    • findStaffByCredentials

      public UserDTO findStaffByCredentials(String username, String password)
      Looks up a staff user by username and password.

      NOTE: this compares the supplied password against the stored value verbatim — real systems must hash and salt passwords; plaintext is accepted here only because this is a teaching prototype. The returned DTO deliberately omits the password so credentials are never sent back to the client.

      Parameters:
      username - the login username
      password - the plaintext password to match against password_hash
      Returns:
      the matching UserDTO, or null if no row matches or the query fails

    • findUserById

      public UserDTO findUserById(long id)
      Looks up a staff user by their primary key.

      Used for server-side authorization: the ClientSession carries only the logged-in actor's id (not their role), so a controller that must gate an operation by role re-reads the user row here. As with findStaffByCredentials(java.lang.String, java.lang.String), the returned DTO omits the password.

      Parameters:
      id - the user identifier to fetch
      Returns:
      the matching UserDTO, or null if no row matches or the query fails

    • findVisitorById

      public VisitorDTO findVisitorById(long id)
      Looks up a visitor by their national ID (login-by-ID).
      Parameters:
      id - the visitor identifier to fetch
      Returns:
      the matching VisitorDTO, or null if no row matches or the query fails

    • lock

      public boolean lock(long actorId, String kind)
      Acquires the single-login lock for an actor by inserting a row into active_session.

      Uses an insert-and-catch strategy rather than check-then-insert: the (actor_id, kind) primary key makes a concurrent second login race fail at the database with a duplicate-key violation, which is caught and reported as false. A check-then-insert could let two logins both pass the check before either inserts.

      Parameters:
      actorId - the user or visitor id to lock
      kind - the actor kind, "USER" or "VISITOR"
      Returns:
      true if the lock was acquired, false if the actor is already locked elsewhere or the insert fails

    • unlock

      public void unlock(long actorId, String kind)
      Releases the single-login lock for an actor by deleting its active_session row. No-op if no row exists.
      Parameters:
      actorId - the user or visitor id to unlock
      kind - the actor kind, "USER" or "VISITOR"
